Global (All).title: Conflict in Ethiopia and Impact on Regional Stability
Global (All).summary: Ongoing clashes in Ethiopia could trigger a broader regional conflict.

Global (All).title: Escalating Tensions between US and China
Global (All).summary: Increased tensions between the US and China pose significant risks to global supply chains, trade and security.

Global (All).title: Protests Against Government Elected in Pakistan
Global (All).summary: Protests against government in Pakistan pose a risk to political stability and security.

United States.title: Ransomware Attack Affects Major US Healthcare Provider
United States.summary: A ransomware attack has affected a major US healthcare provider, exposing sensitive patient data and disrupting medical services.

United States.title: Increased Tensions Between US and China Over Taiwan
United States.summary: The US and China are experiencing increased tensions over Taiwan, with both countries engaging in saber-rattling and diplomatic maneuvering.

United States.title: Civil Unrest in Multiple US Cities Due to Police Brutality Protests
United States.summary: Civil unrest and protests have erupted in multiple US cities due to allegations of police brutality and racial injustice.

South Korea.title: Seoul Mayor Election: Ruling Party Wins, but Opposition Claims Voting Irregularities
South Korea.summary: On June 28, conservative Seoul Mayor Oh Se-hoon was elected as the new Prime Minister of South Korea, but the left-wing opposition party claims widespread voting irregularities and potential election meddling. This could escalate political tensions between the ruling and opposition parties.

South Korea.title: North Korea Fires Three Ballistic Missiles into Sea of Japan
South Korea.summary: On June 26, North Korea fired three ballistic missiles into the Sea of Japan, drawing criticism from the international community. South Korea, along with the US and Japan, condemned the launch and warned North Korea against further military provocations.

South Korea.title: South Korea's Opposition Accuses Government of Failing to Address Growing Inflation
South Korea.summary: On June 24, South Korea's opposition party accused the government of failing to address the growing inflation and economic woes, which has led to increasing public dissatisfaction. This could potentially exacerbate tensions between the ruling and opposition parties.

Japan.title: Japan and South Korea agree to resume talks on forced labor issue
Japan.summary: Japan and South Korea have agreed to resume talks over a dispute about compensation for forced labor during Japan's colonial rule of Korea, South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol said on 26 June. The talks were paused in November 2021 after a South Korean court ordered a Japanese steelmaker to pay compensation to former forced laborers.

Japan.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile, Japan warns citizens to take precautions
Japan.summary: North Korea fired a ballistic missile towards the sea off its east coast on 26 June, South Korean and Japanese militaries said, prompting Japan's government to warn citizens to take precautions.

Japan.title: Japan's PM Fumio Kishida warns of potential 'large-scale' North Korea missile launch
Japan.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida said on 26 June that there was a possibility of a 'large-scale' ballistic missile launch by North Korea, and that Japan was preparing to respond accordingly.
